chapter_title.ar: بَابُ أَنَّ الصَّاعِقَةَ لَا تُصِيبُ ذَاكِراً
chapter_title.en: Chapter 23 – The thunderbolt would not strike a Zakir (Oft-mentioner) of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/135)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/135)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّ الصَّوَاعِقَ لَا تُصِيبُ ذَاكِراً قَالَ قُلْتُ وَ مَا الذَّاكِرُ قَالَ مَنْ قَرَأَ مِائَةَ آيَةٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahi, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ibn Azina, from Bureyd Bin Muawiya Bin Ijaly who said,
‘Abu Abdullahasws said: ‘The thunderbolts will not hit a Zakir (mentioner of Allahazwj)’. I said, ‘And what is the Zakir?’ Heasws said: ‘The one who recites one hundred Verses’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from ibn ‘Udhaynah from Burayd ibn Mu’awiyah al-‘Ijliy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘lightning does not affect the speaker (of Allah).’ I (the narrator) then asked, ‘Who is a speaker?’ The Imam said, ‘One who reads one hundred verses of the Holy Quran.’”
